observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed that the:
Municipal Accountant manually prepare a correct Statement of Cash Flow to ensure the accuracy and fair presentation of financial statements; and
Municipal Accountant issue a correspondence to the accounting software provider pertaining the variances noted in the line items of the Statement of Cash Flows and to necessitate the immediate reprogramming of the software to present an accurate cash flow statement.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed that the:
Municipal Assessor’s Office, Municipal General Services Office and Municipal Engineering Office calculate the cost of the road lots.
Municipal Accountant provide for the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the cost of the road lot as part of the Road Networks (0-07-03-010) account.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed that the:
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile the records pertaining to tax receivables and deferred taxes.
Municipal Accountant provide the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances presented.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed that the:
Municipal Executive Committee formulate procedures in the conduct of inventory count, reconciliation of PPE records and review of reports pertaining to PPE;
Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal General Services Office establish a mechanism to periodically reconcile their records; and
Municipal Mayor’s Office establish a management information system in the Municipal General Services Office for the proper management of supplies and properties.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed that the:
Municipal Accounting Office, Municipal Engineering Office and Municipal General Services Office coordinate with the Inventory Committee in the re-conduct of physical count of on-going projects.
Municipal Accounting Office, Municipal Engineering Office and Municipal General Services Office establish policies and procedures for inter-office reconciliation of records; and,
Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the discrepancies noted.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed that the:
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile their records and trace the transaction based on the transfer date indicated; and
Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the unliquidated fund transfers from various government agencies.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed that the:
Municipal Accounting Office review each transaction recorded under Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and to compare the same with the transactions reflected in each bank records;
Municipal Treasurer’s Office and Municipal Accounting Office devise procedures for the periodic reconciliation of their records to facilitate the monitoring of transfer of funds; and.
Municipal Accountant prepare the adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Budget Office and Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office in the reconciliation of the balances provided in the Notes to the Financial Statements and in the Statement of Financial Position to present a correct balance.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed that the:
Municipal General Services Office and Municipal Accounting Office reconcile their records prior to the preparation of the financial statements to reflect actual drugs and medicines on hand; and
Municipal Accountant prepare the adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the accounts.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed that the:
Municipal Accountant reconcile the details in the payroll against the individual GSIS remittances for the payment of the contribution due for the proper member and to provide for the necessary adjusting entry to present a correct balance; and
Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Human Resource Management Office formulate a review mechanism in the payroll preparation to ensure that premium contributions to GSIS are properly computed, withheld and remitted.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed that the:
Municipal Accountant undertake the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the actual collections reported by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office; and
Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to create a mechanism periodically reconcile records of both offices pertaining to the revenues collected.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed that the:
Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal General Services Office maintain the necessary subsidiary ledger and property cards for the recording of the breeding stocks;
Municipal Accounting Office, Municipal General Services Office and Municipal Agriculture Office account for the increase or decrease due to physical changes and price changes in the market for the period; and
Municipal Accounting Office provide for the necessary adjusting entries to present the correct balance of the said account.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed that the Municipal Accountant provide the necessary disclosures relating to PPE to enable better understanding of financial statement and to facilitate the conduct of reorientation on the provisions of the IPSAS particularly those pertaining to PPE.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed that the:
Municipal Engineer evaluates the records submitted by the contractor and determine the cause of delay and non-compliance with the contract time; and
Municipal Engineering Office devise a mechanism in the monitoring of delays incurred in the projects or through other means that management needs to address the circumstances so requires.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant and the GSO-Designate Officer:
To undertake collaborative effort to trace the noted unreconciled variance of Water Supply System amounting to P 614,661.00;
To re-evaluate the propriety of recording the unreconciled PPE balances under the SEF in consonance with the Criteria on the recognition of PPE specified in Sections 4.1 and 4.2 of the above-mentioned Circular and
Effect corrections/adjusting entries when necessary.

We recommended henceforth that the Municipal Mayor, as chairman of the IC, to mobilize the Committee to prepare a PIP prior to the conduct of physical count of PPE and submit the same to the COA at least 10 days before the scheduled start of the inventory. Also, we recommend that the committee familiarize themselves of COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to heed with the general and procedural guidelines on inventory taking of the PPE accounts.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor, to direct the IC thru the GSO-Designate to effect the necessary changes in the RPCPPE format used by the LGU by incorporating the information prescribed in Annex 49 of the MNGAS Manual and resubmit the report to COA to facilitate an efficient reconciliation and verification of the PPEs.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the IC thru the GSO-Designate to affect the necessary corrections in the RPCPPE to reconcile with List of Non-Existing/Missing PPEs and resubmit a corrected report to the COA.

Furthermore, we recommended the submission of the lacking documents relative to the LGU’s request for the derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs to facilitate the Audit Team’s validation of the claim and to subsequently render a decision on whether or not to grane such request.

We recommended the management to:
Enhance the preparation of the AIP by way of proper planning and specific identification of development projects together with the expected outputs and target period of implementation;
Instruct the Municipal Development Council to come up with a detailed AIP with consultation and deliberation with the officials concerned, specifically require the Punong Barangays to prepare a proposal project before the MDC convene and present the same for approval; and
Ensure that identified development projects will actually address environmental management and socio-economic needs of the constituents, as required by the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 and DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020.

Henceforth, we recommended that the MDRRMC to further enhance its procedures in preparing the MDRRMFIP and ensure that all information required in COA Circular 2012-002 are provided in the plan to maintain transparency and accountability and to facilitate easy evaluation and verification of the identified PPAs.

We henceforth, recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRMO to coordinate with each other to prepare the monthly  Report of Sources and Utilization of the MDRRMF and to comply with the submission on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the COA as required in the above-mentioned Circular and simultaneously, submit the report to the Regional Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (RDRRMC) through the Regional Office of the Civil Defense, copy furnish the Regional Offices of the DILG and DBM as required  in NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to submit a written justification as to why the overpayment of COVID-19 Active Hazard Duty Pay and Special Risk Allowance to these HRHs and PHWs should not be suspended or disallowed in audit and/or require the personnel concerned to refund such payment.

Henceforth, we recommended to the Municipal Mayor to ensure that the LGU strictly adhere to pertinent laws, rules and regulations when granting benefits to personnel in order to maintain efficiency in the utilization of government funds and to avoid irregular expenditures that will be disallowed in audit.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to
Direct the GSO-Designate in coordination with the Municipal Agriculturist to perform physical count of all the breeding stocks to determine existence and actual condition of the assets, especially those that are long been acquired; and
To direct the Municipal Accountant to record adjustments in the books, if necessary, after reconciliation of records with the former offices.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:
Through the MAO, ensure that the LGU prepare and execute a MOA with the recipient farmers and the next in line beneficiaries, clearly stating therein the terms, conditions, duties, responsibilities and benefits in taking custody of the breeding stocks; and
In reiteration of the recommendation in finding “a” of this Memorandum, to direct the GSO-Designate, in coordination with the MAO, to conduct annual physical count of the Breeding Stocks assets of the LGU to determine existence and actual condition of the assets and to facilitate reconciliation with the books, if necessary.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to continue lead the management in all efforts to maximize the utilization of the fund and ensure the achievement of the optimum implementation of projects that will ultimately promote and contribute to the inclusive economic growth and development of the LGU.

We recommended the management to continue in the coming years all the efforts and activities that contribute to the optimum use of MDRRMF and the full implementation of PPAs as identified in the plan in order to continue building and maintaining a disaster resilient Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:

      We recommended the Local Chief Executive to enjoin the following
officials to do the following courses of actions pursuant to COA Circular
2015-008, to wit:

    A. Municipal Accountant

        1. Maintain separate  subsidiary  ledgers  for  the  road  and  its
           components namely: a) road lot, b) road  pavement,  c)  drainage
           and slope protection  structures,  and  d)  other  miscellaneous
           structures.


        2. Reconcile the subsidiary ledger of the road and  its  components
           with  the  Local  Road  Inventory  and  Road   Map   to   ensure
           completeness.

    B.  Municipal Engineer


        1. Segregate and recognize the cost of each component of the  local
           roads with identified cost;


        2. Determine the  components  of  local  roads  identified  in  the
           inventory without corresponding cost and recognize the  cost  of
           each component at its fair value;


        3. Provide the local accountant and the  general  services  officer
           with the complete  description  and  cost  segregation  of  road
           components for road projects.


    C. Municipal General Services Officer


        1. Maintain a Local Road Inventory and  Road  Map  in  coordination
           with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer;


        2. Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for  all  roads
           and its components.

    D. Inventory Committee


        1. Conduct physical count and prepare the Report  on  the  Physical
           Count of the Road Network system.

Recommendations:

      We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:

   1. Direct the Municipal Accountant to  prepare  adjusting  Journal  Entry
      Voucher (JEV) to correct/reclassify the affected accounts; and


   2. Require the Municipal Accountant to recognize unexpired portion of the
      prepaid  insurance  as  prepaid  insurance  and  to  record  only  the
      Insurance Expenses Account upon consummation of the insurance services
      every month thereafter.

Recommendations:

      We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:

     1) Continue its  compliance  with  the  mandatory  provision  for  the
        collection  of  biodegradables,  recyclables,  non-recyclables  and
        special/hazardous wastes; and


     2) Adopt additional  measures  to  fully  enforce  the  Municipal-wide
        observation  of  wastes  diversion  strategies  to  include  source
        reduction, recycling and composting; and


     3) Continue the proper maintenance of the Central  Materials  Recovery
        Facility and as well, enjoin all the Barangay personnel  to  comply
        with the prescribed guidelines on the establishment and maintenance
        of MRF; and
     4)  Conduct  regular  orientations  and/or  trainings  to  all  wastes
        collectors and  personnel  to  ensure  proper  handling  of  wastes
        materials in accordance with the prescribed guidelines.

Recommendations:

      We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:

     1. Thru the Local School Board,  to  formulate  a  three-year  program
        indicating strategic prioritization policies and equitable  sharing
        in the allocation of the SEF to schools  based  on  their  priority
        needs; and


     2. Strictly adhere to the pertinent  provisions  of  applicable  laws,
        rules  and  regulations  governing  SEF  disbursements   to   avoid
        incurring expenses that are not allowed.

Recommendations:

      We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:

   1. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe  the  deadlines  in
      the remittance of the provincial share of the SEF collection which  is
      within 15 days of every succeeding month and to revisit RA No. 5447 to
      familiarize herself  with  the  duties  and  responsibilities  in  the
      handling of SEF collections; and


   2. Maximize and improve the utilization of the ECPAC system in  order  to
      ultimately improve the overall efficiency in  the  revenue  generation
      and financial reporting system of the Municipality.

Recommendations:

      We recommended the Municipal Accountant to:

     a.  Maintain  a  complete  set   of   subsidiary   ledgers   for   the
        aforementioned special accounts in the General Fund; and


     b. Prepare separate financial statements thereof  in  accordance  with
        the provisions of Section 313 of  RA  7160  and  the  NGAS  Manual,
        Volume 1.

Recommendations:

      We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:

     1. Prioritize the implementation of the projects/programs lined up  in
        the AIP for the calendar year.


     2. Ensure that the Municipality’s equipment are mostly  available  and
        that mechanical issues are addressed promptly.


     3. Finally, direct the  Local  Development  Council  (LDC)  and  other
        appropriate  offices  to  re-assess  and  re-evaluate  the  current
        processes being  undertaken  by  the  Municipality,  including  the
        process on the liquidations, in order that appropriate changes will
        be made so that projects and/or programs that are lined up  in  the
        AIP will be fully implemented and eventual benefits are felt by the
        constituents.

Recommendations:

      We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:


     a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to maximize the use of the  features
        embedded in the computerized system particularly for collection and
        deposit so that complete and  accurate  data  will  be  transferred
        and/or carried to the Office of the Municipal Accountant during the
        period.


     b. Advise the Municipal Accountant to properly review the  transferred
        data against the duplicate copy  of  the  receipts  issued  to  the
        payees and effect adjustments,  if  any,  to  reflect  the  correct
        account balances.

     c. Create a team that will be responsible for the close monitoring  of
        the full implementation  of  the  computerization  project  of  the
        Municipality.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to: Advise and strictly monitor the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections of the Municipality promptly and intact to AGDB; Instruct the Municipal Accountant to regularly prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements to preclude accumulation of reconciling items from both the bank and the books. To facilitate timely preparation of BRS, the municipality can avail the “We Access” facilities of the LandBank where it can generate information from the bank accounts maintained right from their own computers without waiting for the release of the bank statement; Direct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all checks issued for the month are included in the RCI and reported in chronological sequence. The RCI should be submitted to the Accounting Office with complete DVs; Establish a policy that will hasten the return of the DVs from the concerned departments to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer after being paid to prevent delay in the submission of RCI with paid DVs to the accounting office; Direct the Municipal Accountant to stop the manner of recording only the RCI with attached DVs. Such practice counters management’s assertion on completeness of assets, income and expenses. Immediately effect the necessary adjusting entries of the reconciling items duly supported with relevant documents in order to reflect the correct balances of the Cash in Bank accounts and the related income and expenses; Instruct the former designated Municipal Accountant to render an accounting of the long outstanding checks which were identified and certified in the BRS during her time. A timeline shall be established to ensure completion of the task which eventually facilitate in the adjustment of reconciling items; and Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to determine the inactive bank accounts and cause the closure of these accounts to preclude the charges imposed by the banks.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Instructed the Municipal Accountant to facilitate timely preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements and stop the manner of recording only the Report of Check Issued (RCI) with attached DVs and to immediately effect the necessary adjusting entries of the reconciling items duly supported with relevant documents in order to reflect the correct balances of the Cash in Bank accounts and the related income and expenses. Further ordered the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to determine the inactive bank accounts and cause the closure of these accounts to preclude the charges imposed by the banks.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Accounting Department that purchase of supplies and materials for stock, regardless of whether or not they are consumed within the accounting period, shall be recorded as inventory following the perpetual inventory method. Under the perpetual inventory method, an inventory control account is maintained in the General Ledger on a current basis. In addition, detailed inventory records are maintained for each inventory item. To avoid maintenance of inventory accounts of consumed supplies and materials, the Local Treasurer, shall consolidate weekly the Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) for which supplies and materials were issued using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI). The SMMI together with the original copy of the RIS shall be submitted to the Chief Accountant as basis of recording the consumed supplies and materials as expense.

        Development Office to closely coordinate with each other  in  order
        to strictly comply with the guidelines provided in the NDRRMC,  DBM
        and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No.  2013-1  and  integrate  the
        programs and activities for the LDRRM in the Annual Investment Plan
        (AIP) of the Municipality before its final approval;


     b. Direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer
        to ensure that the CY 2019 LDRRMFIP used by the Municipality is  in
        accordance with the format  prescribed  in  COA  Circular  2012-002
        dated September 12, 2012; and


     c. Require also the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction  and  Management
        Officer to attend trainings,  briefings  and/or  orientations  with
        agencies such as DILG,  NDRRMC  and  DBM  to  further  enhance  his
        understanding of LDRRM, particularly on the preparation  of  proper
        LDRRMFIP and familiarity of some forms.

        address  the  deficiencies  cited  above  correcting/repairing  the

        Order No. 11, series of 2014 in the construction of farm to  market
        roads.

        connected with the LGU; and

     c. Stop the practice of granting additional cash  advance  unless  the
        previous cash advance is accounted/liquidated.

timeliness in the payment of salaries for casuals and job orders as well  as
the remittances to address the deficiency  of  delayed  remittance  of  HDMF
monthly contribution.

Recommendations:

        guidelines; and

Instruct the Accounting Department that  purchase  of supplies and materials for  stock, regardless  of  whether  or  not they  are consumed  within  the  accounting  period, shall be recorded   as  inventory following   the   perpetual inventory method.  Under the perpetual inventory method, an inventory control account is maintained in the General Ledger on a current basis.
